summary: This cluster focuses on the discovery and taxonomic characterization of the freshwater red alga *Caloglossa beccarii* in riverine ecosystems of Kerala, India. Researchers identified the species in the Periyar and Chalakkudy rivers, documenting its tetrasporangiate life stage and spore discharge mechanisms, which indicate a close morphological relationship to *Caloglossa fluviatilis*. The studies emphasize the species’ leaf shape and rhizoid arrangement to confirm its placement within the *Caloglossa* genus, while also mapping its broader geographic distribution across southeast Asia, including India, Burma, Malaysia, Indonesia, and Australia.
entity_sample: Szechwan Province near Peh-pei on the Jialing River
